results.question: What happens when an assignment notice is sent to the insurer?

results.llm_answer: When a written notice is sent to the insurer, the assignment becomes effective. The insurer does not guarantee the validity of the assignment.

results.llm_answer: No, the insurer cannot require evidence of insurability when the insured converts this policy to a permanent policy.

results.question: If the insured dies during the grace period, will the death benefit be reduced?
results.expected_answer: Coverage continues during the grace period per Section 6.2, and the full death benefit is payable under Section 5.1 if the insured dies while the policy is in force; the policy remains in force during the grace period, so the full death benefit would be payable.
results.evidence: 6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period. / 5.1 We will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if: (a) The insured dies while this policy is in force
results.llm_answer: The death benefit will not be reduced if the insured dies during the grace period, as coverage remains active and the policy is still in force.

results.question: If death occurs after the policy has been in force for three years due to a cause other than suicide, will the insurer be able to contest the claim on the basis of misrepresentation in the application?
results.expected_answer: No. The incontestability clause prevents the insurer from contesting the policy after two years in force during the insured's lifetime, and suicide is the only stated exclusion.
results.evidence: 10.1 INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two years from the issue date. / 7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.
results.llm_answer: Yes, the insurer will not be able to contest the claim on the basis of misrepresentation because the policy has been in force for more than two years, making it incontestable.
